Gloves of Stability  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HANDS
AC: 35
STR: +7 STA: +7 HP: +75
SV FIRE: +8 SV DISEASE: +8 SV COLD: +8 SV MAGIC: +8 SV POISON: +8
WT: 20.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Gloves of Stability
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HANDS
AC: 35
STR: +7 STA: +7 HP: +75
SV FIRE: +8 SV DISEASE: +8 SV COLD: +8 SV MAGIC: +8 SV POISON: +8
WT: 20.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):60, 60, 60
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Dark Horizons
Lucy Entry By:Berien
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 10:02:32
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Temple of Veeshan
NPC Name
Lendiniara the Keeper


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Temple of Veeshan


Uploaded March 6th, 2022 by halfridge
Updated March 7th, 2022

Post Comment
RPS
QuoteReply
# Mar 11 2004 at 8:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259747245
29 posts
Bottom line, most guild killing first broods are probly on an RPS, so nothing is going to be "given" to anyone. if a monk wants to spend RPS on it, then they can.
▲ Page top
why would monks want them anyway
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2003 at 2:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781123020
44 posts

ignoring the 20 wt even if they was 2.0 wt i would think monks would prefer Gods or even epic over theese. Im unsure but i think monk epics are 100hp anyhow. After looking for gloves for my ranger I would take theese honestly over Yelis head ( for Gods ). for one reason the claw in ssra is hands equipabel and I dont want to have haste on my hands. My reason is simple get claw from Ssra, acrylia broad sword from Emp. ( most guilds give theese to rangers what i noticed. on my server I have yet to see a rogue or bard with one). Agnarr drops a 17 20 sword which a ranger also has a great chance to get leaving the hands open for claw.

Now i know a lot of people are saying WTF you talking about rangers dont get those weapons. What I seen on the ssra weapons they do. Rogues who are using the backstab mod piercers in each hand, warriors get anything that has agro proc on it. Which leaves the agnar sword to rangers and bards but most bards use epic in offhand for the proc to help DPS, and get a lot of decent weapons in ssra. So rangers do have a decent chance for that sword too, as well as the 14 18 one ( not the one with the stun proc )
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 25 2002 at 3:24 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Guess what. This IS NOT a monk item.
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 11 2003 at 10:55 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) it is if he as especially light gear, besides, its gotta hell of alot of AC on it, so with the 7str its 13wt, ac could make up for that a little. Anyway Yoda, Thanks for the input.
RE: Monk item.
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2003 at 10:59 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591779699212
16 posts
No Shemfade. Monks are limited on weight unlike other classes. Their STR doesn't matter.
▲ Page top
#Diepen, Posted: Dec 04 2002 at 7:04 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Seems kinda stupid to have an item usable by monks that weighs more than any monk can carry in total without a stat penalty.
Gloves
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2002 at 5:29 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592757253151
2 posts
Lendiniara the Keeper (Temple of Veeshan) is found in the West Wing. Its a First Brood dragon, so drops a nice Talisman along with some other nice goodies.

The gloves are a black colour and go nicely with the rest of my rogue oufit.

Clouded One (Old Continent) Xev

# Jun 21 2001 at 11:43 AM Rating: Default

What a great item I only wish a wussy little High Elf clric could where this without being way over weight. Oh well but I would give big congratz to anyone who had these.

13 posts
Why would a high elf cleric want these? If you are capable of getting these, get Gauntlets of Dragon Slaying..
▲ Page top
one little question...
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 10:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751586357
View User Forum
12 posts
why is it that every single item with somewhat uber stats gets a comment on it "this would be awsome for monks!" followed by some complaint about the one shortcomming of the item... usually weight... followed by a list of flames...?

i would love, for once, to read the comments on an item, and actually be told some useful information about what drops it and where, what the proc does, etc, rather than a long flame started by one guy (or girl) who aparantly has a bad case of item envy... it gets old after a while, ya know?


*shrug* just my two cents
▲ Page top
Heavy
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 9:42 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965902610
View User Forum
Scholar
29 posts
Nice gloves, a little heavy for anyone but a warrior, or big race with a lot of STR

RE: Heavy
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 7:27 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344063207
View User Forum
177 posts
Actually my level 40 ranger is using imbued granite spaulders, 19.4 wt and his wt is only at 104 out of 165 with some heavy bags and items in his inventory, so 20 wt isnt that much and for any melee'r isnt bad unless for monks
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 20 2001 at 8:56 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) MULLEN!!
Time and Time again
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 8:20 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591753745587
Scholar
16 posts
Why is it that every other item in this database has a running thread argueing about who should get it? The only people who dont post are the people who actually go and GET it.

None of the Uber guilds seem to have a problem in distributing the items they get, its only the people who stare at these items and wish they could go and get them. Sad, just sad.

Synopsys of 90% of Postings
Player_001: This would be a graet monk item!
Player_002: Your retarded, this should go to a hybrid, any monk rolling on this should be banned
Player_001: STFU!
Player_002: You STFU! Don't talk about a class you haven't played to 60, you moron
Player_003: Uhhh, anyone know who drops this?
Player_002: Oh, and nice spelling of "great" jerkface, didn't your momma send you to school?
Player_001: Looks who is talking, Mr. Grammer, "your are" is contracted as "you're" not "your", so please shut your pie hole!
Player_004: I think its dropped in Veshan's Peak, if you go in the entrance and go to the left...<continues with pertinant information>
Player_005: What, druids are screwed again? (*&#$(*# Verant!
Player_006: All classes and races are viable, please post useful information instead of ***********
Player_001: STFU!
Player_002: STFU!
Player_003: Oh brother...I'm outta here.
Player_004: Me too!
Player_005: lol, me owz joo l33t!
Player_006: I should have stayed in UO, sigh

<Ad Nauseum >

I gotta start rating more posts so I dont have to see this junk any more, ack!

Mullen Skywatcher
45th Level Port-a-Puppy
<Still thinks Forest Loops are Uber Loot>

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 21 2001 at 12:21 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Mullen, AWESOME post. I must say, that is the best post i've seen on allakhazams.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 20 2001 at 12:36 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ROFL
RE: Time and Time again
QuoteReply
# Jun 21 2001 at 9:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591753745587
Scholar
16 posts
Heh, thanks for all the positive feedback, I was getting really frustrated there! I get annoyed with people who post negative B.S. on this site because:

1. The site is awesome. My home page at work and at home (BTW, this sites hosts EQAtlas too!).

2. There are some really great posters here, people who take things that are very, very difficult to find out, and post them so that everyone has an easier time of it. I am not one of these people, but I am decent at mocking others, so I still allowed in the sandbox...

Please take the time to thank the REAL posters, i.e. the people who post information that helps you, and I'll keep my eyes open for more demons of stupidity to exercise!

P.S Illa, after posting this, gonna run to paypal for another donation. I didn't find out until recently that you also host EQAtlas, another great site (and also the bane of my work laser printers). Also, the last two interview with Curt Schilling and R.A. Salvatore were worth another donation just for those alone. Keep up the good work!

Mullen Skywatcher
45th Level Port-a-Puppy
Port Slave of Vita
Sacred Knights of Norrath
Bertoxx

Beta Tester of Velious Suicide Spell: aka Wolfform
You begin casting Wolfform
You are now a wolf.
A coldain wolfmaster hits you for 383 points of damage
A coldain wolfmaster hits you for 127 points of damage
A coldain wolfmaster hits you for 227 points of damage
You have been slain by a coldain wolfmaster!
NOW LOADING....

(I guess experience is the best teacher, but then why do I lose it when I die??) :)

Keep those posts coming!

I am surprised that any monk aruges that this is good for them. Even if they weighed nothing, they are still useless to a monk of apropriate level to attain them. Your EPICS are gloves.

# Jul 12 2001 at 5:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248017854
3 posts
With my monk I would use this as a resist item only, until I got my Epic. I haven&#8217;t tried going 18 over WT (16 WT now) but I would probably lose more than 20 AC, my gloves now have 8 AC. If I break even on AC the 75 HP and other stats would be nice.

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 19 2001 at 8:31 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If you think that this is a monk item,
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 19 2001 at 8:26 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) best class stuff is BS....any class that wants high Resist gloves should love those
#QrowMagnon, Posted: Jun 19 2001 at 5:54 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Best class for these gloves is Warrior. Near perfect stats for what a Warrior would want.
Ummm.....NO!
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 6:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757823993
9 posts
The best class for these is, IMO, all the melee classes there (not including monk, since these ARE a bit heavy). These have VERY good rogue stats, rogues need the stamina for heavier weapons in long fights. The strength for the backstabs. The hitpoints because, as a 50+ rogue, I know that the warrior's taunt is not always enough and sometimes the evade isn't either. And the resists are always nice too in case there is a dmg shield or AE spells or whatever. All classes need resists.
▲ Page top
In response
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 8:12 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246074671
47 posts
Three things.

1: This was made for ANY of the classes listed.
2: Between a rogue and a warrior i would happily give AC and resistance to a warrior over a rogue (no offense to rogues, but warriors are front line post 50)
3: Although a monk COULD use these, the weight just puts it way outa worth.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 20 2001 at 10:56 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) do you think that a rogue is not front line in fights? do you have any idea how easy it is, as a rogue, to out taunt a warrior?
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 20 2001 at 4:40 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If a 50+ rogue is wielding a heavy weapon, there's something wrong about him. 8)
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 19 2001 at 4:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) One pair of really, really heavy frikkin' gloves. Oh well, monks.. You know you want 'em, but do you wanna really waste 20.0wt on 'em? Didn't think so. Awesome for about any class listed.. EVEN monks. Since the new weight increase you guys get. And no, I don't play a monk, and no, I don't diss/love monks. Just saying, these are kickass gloves from a place I'll never be =P
